profile.title: The Initiated Reader
profile.summary: Your instincts are already moving beyond surface symbolism. You understand that position shapes meaning, that cards in combination create narrative, and that the useful question is rarely what a card "means" in isolation.
profile.startPoint: Begin directly with spread practice. The Minor Arcana will be your focus — you don't need the Major Arcana foundations.

spread.name: Three Card
spread.description: Past, Present, Future — the spine of any reading.

card.name: The Chariot
card.upright: Justice holds a sword and scales — clarity and balance. This is not poetic justice but the logical consequence of actions taken. In a reading it often points to legal matters, contracts, or situations where fairness and accountability are at stake. More broadly, it asks you to look honestly at your own conduct: what cause have you set in motion, and what effect are you prepared to meet?
card.reversed: Dishonesty, unfair treatment, avoiding accountability. Or a situation where justice is genuinely absent — and you know it.
card.relational: Next to The Emperor: institutional justice, law. Next to The Moon: deception clouding a fair outcome. Next to The Eight of Swords: self-imposed restriction from a fear of judgement.

scenario.love: A relationship requiring honesty about what is and isn't fair to each person.
scenario.work: Contracts, negotiations, performance reviews, or the consequences of past decisions arriving.
scenario.self: Where are you not being fully honest with yourself or others?

card.name: The Death
card.upright: Death rarely means death. In nearly every context it signals an ending that makes a beginning possible — the kind of change that is so complete there is no going back. Something has run its course. The grief is real, but so is what comes after. In a reading, Death asks what you are clinging to that has already ended, and what would become possible if you let it go.
card.reversed: Resistance to necessary change, clinging to what is already over. Or stagnation so complete that even ending has stalled.
card.relational: Next to The Fool: profound transformation leading to a new beginning. Next to The Tower: sudden, devastating change. Next to The Ten of Cups: the end of a chapter but not of love.

scenario.love: A relationship that has fundamentally changed, or needs to end for both people to grow.
scenario.work: The end of a role, a business phase, or an identity tied to work.
scenario.self: What in your sense of self needs to be allowed to die so something real can replace it?

card.name: The Chariot
card.upright: Two sphinxes pull the chariot in opposite directions — one black, one white. The Charioteer holds no reins. He controls them through pure will and focused intention. This is the card of hard-won forward movement, of harnessing opposing forces and driving them toward a goal. Ambition, determination, and the refusal to be pulled apart.
card.reversed: Loss of direction, scattered energy, a vehicle with no driver. Reversed, The Chariot can also signal aggression used as a substitute for clarity.
card.relational: Next to Strength: willpower meeting patience — powerful. Next to The Moon: direction lost in confusion. Next to The Wheel: momentum working with or against you.

scenario.love: A relationship moving forward despite obstacles — or a person who is hard to keep up with.
scenario.work: Drive and ambition in full force. The ability to push through opposition.
scenario.self: What opposing forces in yourself are you trying to reconcile, and are you doing it with force or with mastery?
